title: SOC 2 CC7 — Unauthorized Production Configuration Change Detection

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOC 2 CC7.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 CC7.2 (System monitoring for anomalies) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.8.20: SOC 2 CC7 — Unauthorized Production Configuration Change Detection.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.8.20' running on sourcetype aws:cloudtrail and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows or Palo Alto Networks (as applicable)
spl:
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventName IN ("AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress","RevokeSecurityGroupIngress","ModifyDBClusterParameterGroup") earliest=-7d
| eval change_key=md5(_raw)
| join type=left change_key [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-14d
    | eval change_key=md5(number+cmdb_ci)
    | fields number
  ]
| where isnull(number)
| stats count by eventName, userIdentity.arn
| sort - count
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Change data model (Change.All_Changes).
description: CC7 addresses system operations. Detecting changes to firewall rules, cloud security groups, or database parameters without a matching approved change ticket demonstrates monitoring over unauthorized modifications.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes aws, itsm and sourcetypes aws:cloudtrail, snow:change_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: addresses system operations — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Replace simplified join with CMDB-linked correlation on `requestParameters.groupId`; (2) allow emergency change workflow via `userIdentity.arn` lookup `breakglass_arns.csv`; (3) send to SOC and ITIL process owner; (4) retain CloudTrail in WORM bucket; (5) map events to CC7-2 subservice in system description. Indexes required: `index=aws`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail`, `sourcetype=snow:change_request`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows or Palo Alto Networks (as applicable).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` (eventName, requestParameters, userIdentity.arn, _time); `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, start_date, cmdb_ci).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Replace simplified join with CMDB-linked correlation on `requestParameters.groupId`; (2) allow emergency change workflow via `userIdentity.arn` lookup `breakglass_arns.csv`; (3) send to SOC and ITIL process owner; (4) retain CloudTrail in WORM bucket; (5) map events to CC7-2 subservice in system description.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventName IN ("AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress","RevokeSecurityGroupIngress","ModifyDBClusterParameterGroup") earliest=-7d
| eval change_key=md5(_raw)
| join type=left change_key [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-14d
    | eval change_key=md5(number+cmdb_ci)
    | fields number
  ]
| where isnull(number)
| stats count by eventName, userIdentity.arn
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC7 — Unauthorized Production Configuration Change Detection** — CC7 addresses system operations. Detecting changes to firewall rules, cloud security groups, or database parameters without a matching approved change ticket demonstrates monitoring over unauthorized modifications.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` (eventName, requestParameters, userIdentity.arn, _time); `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, start_date, cmdb_ci). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows or Palo Alto Networks (as applicable). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=aws, sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **change_key** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(number)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **eventName, userIdentity.arn**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**SOC 2 CC7 — Unauthorized Production Configuration Change Detection** — CC7 addresses system operations. Detecting changes to firewall rules, cloud security groups, or database parameters without a matching approved change ticket demonstrates monitoring over unauthorized modifications.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` (eventName, requestParameters, userIdentity.arn, _time); `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` (number, start_date, cmdb_ci). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows or Palo Alto Networks (as applicable). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: change_key, eventName, userIdentity.arn.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unmatched changes), Timeline, Single value (count).
visualization: Table (unmatched changes), Timeline, Single value (count). Key fields: `change_key`, `eventName`, `userIdentity.arn`.
knownFalsePositives: Control test failures during audit windows, immature control rollouts, or known compensating-control gaps.
